topic_scope.detail: The cycle-1 scope_creep failure ('embeddings' defined with an appositive clause in §7) is resolved. The revised §7 'Word vectors in language models' subsection contains a single sentence: 'This principle extends to word vectors in language models, covered in a later module [1].' No defining clause, no elaboration — compliant with name-and-defer form. 'Cosine similarity' and 'distance metrics' appear once in the §5.4 distance_as_similarity term definition as deferred items ('introduced in later topics') — no definition given. Nearest-neighbour search, clustering, and classification each appear in one bullet in §5.5 with a collective defer at line 158. Dot product and distance formula do not appear. No concept is taught beyond the topic's headline scope of coordinate axes, geometric interpretation of vectors, music-taste example, and distance-as-similarity intuition.

beginner_readability.detail: All new terms bold-defined on first use using the 'bold term — plain meaning' format: coordinate (§5.1), axis (§5.1, within coordinate definition), origin (§5.1), coordinate plane (§5.2), point in space (§5.2), 3D coordinate space (§5.3), coordinate space (§5.6), N-dimensional space (§5.6), neighbourhood (§5.6). No paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms simultaneously — §5.6 presents three terms but in separate definition blocks. No hedging phrases detected. Active voice, second person ('you') throughout. Sentences are concise (predominantly under 20 words). The Alice/Bob/Carol running example provides concrete grounding before abstraction in every subsection. No undefined acronyms. 0 soft findings — readability check: PASS.

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks pass. Cycle 2 fixes confirmed: (1) length expanded from ~1,850w to ~2,980w, placing the corpus at the standard-profile minimum boundary (0.7% below 3,000w, well within the 30% hard-fail threshold of 2,100w); (2) the 'embeddings' forward reference and scope_creep in §7 replaced with a single compliant name-and-defer sentence ('This principle extends to word vectors in language models, covered in a later module'). Citation density maintained at ≥60% for §§5–7 with primary resource [1] cited 7 times. Sequence drift: clean — no unresolved forward references. Topic scope: clean — no out-of-scope concepts taught. Beginner readability: strong — all terms defined on first use, no jargon dumps, no hedging. Corpus is cleared to ground downstream artifacts.
